bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening since there is no time pressure yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, with concessions only on issueD and issueE, so I will keep issueD as valueB since it is equally best for me while holding firm on the other high-value issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me rather than conceding, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while preserving my utility, I switch issueD from valueB to valueA, which is equally preferred by me.

At this very early stage (t=0.04), I am still targeting near-maximal utility and should not concede much, especially on the high-weight issues A, B, C, and D. The opponent has repeated the same offer three times, which gives me only a limited signal: they may care about issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, both of which are acceptable or ideal for me. I will therefore keep my top values while using issueD=valueB, which aligns with their repeated bids and avoids exactly repeating my immediately previous offer.

Their bids suggest they may care about issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, but issueD is equally good for me as valueA or valueB, so I can vary that without cost while holding firm on my high-importance issues.
